---
title: "Module 3: Control Flow and Functions"
---

## Module Overview

This module teaches you how to control the flow of your programs using conditional statements and loops, and how to write reusable code with functions.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, you will be able to:

- Write conditional statements using if, elif, and else
- Use comparison and logical operators in conditions
- Implement different types of loops (for and while)
- Control loop execution with break and continue
- Define and call functions
- Use parameters, arguments, and return values
- Understand variable scope
- Write functions with default parameters and variable arguments

## Module Contents

### Lesson 1: Conditional Statements
Learn how to make decisions in your code based on conditions.

### Lesson 2: Loops
Master iteration techniques for repeating code efficiently.

### Lesson 3: Functions
Create reusable blocks of code with functions.

## Prerequisites

You should have completed Modules 1 and 2 and be comfortable with:
- Variables and data types
- Basic operations
- Data structures (lists, dictionaries, etc.)

::: {.callout-tip}
## Control Flow in Mathematics

Control flow is essential for mathematical programming:
- Implementing algorithms step-by-step
- Processing sequences of numbers
- Making decisions based on mathematical conditions
- Creating reusable mathematical functions
:::

## Estimated Time

This module should take approximately 6-8 hours to complete, including exercises and practice time.

## Ready to Start?

Begin with [Lesson 1: Conditional Statements](lesson1.qmd)!
